rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for port-level assumptions/guarantees, implementations for subcomponent bindings.
AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and should not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Component type contracts should reference only visible features and not subcomponent paths or implementation-specific details.
Ensure AGREE type declarations match AADL data types: int, real, bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` within `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `eq` for helper declarations and `const` for named constants in AGREE contracts.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees or assignments in component type annexes; move them to implementation scopes.
Avoid referencing package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties (e.g., Deadline, Period) directly in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of component structure
